name: Terry Gibbs
abstract: Terry Gibbs (born Julius Gubenko; October 13, 1924) is an American jazz vibraphonist and band leader. Gibbs has performed or recorded with Tommy Dorsey, Chubby Jackson, Buddy Rich, Woody Herman, Benny Goodman, Alice Coltrane, Louie Bellson, Charlie Shavers, Mel Tormé, Buddy DeFranco, and others.

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Jolly
about_quotes.evidence: Well I did one album with Terry Gibbs. “Terry Gibbs Plays The Duke.”

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Goodwin
about_quotes.evidence: so what happened — oh it’s still a blur — so much was going on and L.A. was just rife with things to do. Lots of gigs, lots of jam sessions, lots of rehearsals. about_quotes.evidence: It still seems like you’re doing – it says here you did arrangements for a number of television series... about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: I rehearsed at his house two or three days... I said, 'Look, Terry, pay me what you want.'... You mentioned Eugene Maslov... Then Kenny Burrell is listed on here... Then Finding the Groove, Terry Gibbs, 'with special guest Hubert Laws.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Shew
about_quotes.evidence: Terry, instead of calling one of the other guys in L.A. he thought I’m going to call that Bobby Shew guy and see if he’ll do these shows. So he called me up in Vegas and he said, “Can you come down to L.A. and do these?”
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Shew interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 1, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: I took a leave of absence from this show that I was doing with Terry Gibbs... re-joined Terry Gibbs on this Operation Entertainment.
